event.title: Woodstock Music Festival
event.description: A three-day music festival that became a defining moment of the 1960s counterculture movement, attracting over 400,000 people to a dairy farm.

event.title: Fall of the Berlin Wall
event.description: The opening of the Berlin Wall marked the symbolic end of the Cold War and the beginning of German reunification.

event.title: Construction of the Great Pyramid
event.description: The Great Pyramid of Giza was built as a tomb for Pharaoh Khufu, remaining the tallest man-made structure for over 3,800 years.

event.title: Atomic Bombing of Hiroshima
event.description: The first nuclear weapon used in warfare was dropped on Hiroshima, leading to Japan's surrender and the end of World War II.

event.title: First Powered Flight
event.description: The Wright Brothers achieved the first sustained, controlled, powered heavier-than-air flight on a sandy strip of land.

event.title: Fall of Tenochtitlan
event.description: Spanish conquistador Hernán Cortés and his allies besieged and conquered the Aztec capital, one of the largest cities in the world at the time.

event.title: Apollo 11 Moon Landing Launch
event.description: Apollo 11 launched from this site carrying the first humans to walk on the Moon.

event.title: Eruption of Mount Vesuvius
event.description: Mount Vesuvius erupted catastrophically, burying the Roman cities of Pompeii and Herculaneum under volcanic ash and pumice.

event.title: Signing of the Declaration of Independence
event.description: Representatives of the thirteen American colonies adopted the Declaration of Independence, asserting sovereignty from Britain.

event.title: Completion of the Taj Mahal
event.description: Mughal Emperor Shah Jahan completed this ivory-white marble mausoleum as a memorial to his late wife Mumtaz Mahal.

event.title: D-Day Invasion
event.description: Allied forces launched the largest seaborne invasion in history on the beaches of Normandy, opening a second front in WWII Europe.

event.title: Chernobyl Nuclear Disaster
event.description: Reactor No. 4 at the Chernobyl Nuclear Power Plant exploded during a safety test, causing the worst nuclear disaster in history.

event.title: Construction of Machu Picchu
event.description: The Inca emperor Pachacuti built this mountaintop citadel as a royal estate high in the Andes.

event.title: Signing of the Magna Carta
event.description: King John of England was forced to sign this charter of rights, establishing the principle that everyone, including the king, was subject to law.

event.title: First Reports of COVID-19
event.description: Health authorities identified a cluster of pneumonia cases caused by a novel coronavirus, which would become a global pandemic.

event.title: Nelson Mandela Released from Prison
event.description: After 27 years of imprisonment, anti-apartheid leader Nelson Mandela walked free, leading to the end of apartheid.

event.title: Fall of Constantinople
event.description: Ottoman Sultan Mehmed II conquered the Byzantine capital, ending the Roman Empire after over 1,500 years and reshaping the Mediterranean world.

event.title: Tunguska Event
event.description: A massive explosion flattened 2,000 square kilometers of Siberian forest, believed to have been caused by an airburst of a meteor or comet fragment.

event.title: Darwin's Visit to the Galápagos
event.description: Charles Darwin arrived at these volcanic islands aboard HMS Beagle, where observations of unique wildlife would later inspire his theory of evolution.

event.title: Roswell UFO Incident
event.description: A rancher discovered unusual debris on his property, sparking decades of UFO conspiracy theories after the military initially reported recovering a 'flying disc.'

event.title: Creation of the Moai Statues
event.description: The Rapa Nui people carved and transported nearly 900 massive stone statues across the island, an engineering feat that still mystifies researchers.

event.title: Creation of the Lascaux Cave Paintings
event.description: Prehistoric humans created stunning cave paintings of animals and geometric shapes, among the finest examples of Paleolithic art ever discovered.

event.title: The Hijra - Birth of the Islamic Calendar
event.description: Prophet Muhammad and his followers migrated from Mecca to Medina, an event so significant it marks year one of the Islamic calendar.

event.title: Signing of the Treaty of Versailles
event.description: The treaty formally ending World War I was signed in the Hall of Mirrors, imposing heavy reparations on Germany and redrawing European borders.

event.title: Tiananmen Square Protests
event.description: Pro-democracy protesters occupied the largest public square for weeks before a military crackdown that drew worldwide condemnation.
